summary: Proparacaine starts acting after approximately 2-5 minutes when used as a topical anesthesia for ophthalmic procedures or diagnostic tests involving eye drops and contact lenses. Its effects are generally short-lived due to its rapid metabolism by pseudocholinesterase enzymes present at high levels around ocular tissues. To ensure adequate analgesia during these brief interventions, it is often administered just before initiating such tasks requiring patient comfort without sensation.
